Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1. **Analyze the Request:** *   **Input:** Two parts describing a loan application process: Part 1 (Strict Normative Constraints/Sequence) and Part 2 (Desiderata/Soft Recommendations). Plus a tabular Event Log with 3 cases. *   **Output Requirements:** 1. Identify normative rule violations (which cases and why). 2. Identify desiderata failures (which cases, noting they arent severe, just suboptimal). 3. Suggest interpretations/justifications for the observed behaviors. *   **Tone/Style:** Objective, analytical, structured.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 2.  **Analyze Part 1: Normative Rules:** *   *Sequence:* Application Received -> Credit Check -> Risk Assessment -> Loan Decision -> Inform Applicant -> [If Approved: Finalize Documentation] -> Close Case *   *Ordering Rules:* *   Credit Check before Risk Assessment. *   Risk Assessment before Loan Decision. *   Inform Applicant after Loan Decision. *   Approved -> Finalize Documentation before Close Case. *   Rejected -> do NOT finalize documentation before closing. *   *Not Allowed:* Skipping, changing order, finalizing after rejection, closing without following decision logic.

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: 3.  **Analyze Part 2: Desiderata (Soft Rules):** *   Credit Check within 24 hours of Application Received. *   Inform Applicant within 48 hours of Loan Decision. *   Follow-up Call before Close Case.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4.  **Evaluate the Event Log Case by Case:** *   **Case 1:** *   *Events:* App Rec (09:00) -> Credit Check (09:30) -> Risk Assessment (10:00) -> Loan Decision Approved (11:00) -> Inform Applicant (13:00) -> Finalize Doc (13:30) -> Close Case (14:00). All on 2024-01-10. *   *Normative Check:* Perfect sequence. Approved path followed correctly (Finalize before Close). *   *Desiderata Check:* *   Credit Check within 24h? Yes (30 mins). *   Inform within 48h of Decision? Yes (2 hours). *   Follow-up Call? No (not in the log). *   *Result:* No normative violations. One desideratum failure (missing follow-up call).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: *   **Case 2:** *   *Events:* App Rec (08:00) -> Risk Assessment (08:20) -> Credit Check (09:00) -> Loan Decision Rejected (09:30) -> Finalize Doc (10:00) -> Close Case (10:30). All on 2024-01-11. *   *Normative Check:* *   Risk Assessment (08:20) happened *before* Credit Check (09:00). -> **VIOLATION** (Ordering rule: CC must occur before RA). *   Loan Decision is Rejected (09:30), but Finalize Documentation (10:00) occurred. -> **VIOLATION** (Rule: If rejected, do not finalize documentation). *   *Desiderata Check:* *   Credit Check within 24h? Yes (1 hour). *   Inform within 48h of Decision? Inform Applicant is missing from the log entirely! Wait, is Inform Applicant a strict rule or desideratum? Looking at Part 1: Inform Applicant must occur after Loan Decision. under Ordering Rules. And under Sequence: Loan Decision -> Inform Applicant. The log doesnt show Inform Applicant for Case 2. This is a **Normative Violation** (Skipping a required activity in the sequence). *Self-correction: I must include this.* *   Follow-up Call? No (not in log). *   *Result:* Multiple normative violations (ordering, rejected logic, skipping mandatory step). Desiderata failure (missing follow-up call).

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: *   **Case 3:** *   *Events:* App Rec (Jan 12, 09:00) -> Credit Check (Jan 14, 10:00) -> Risk Assessment (Jan 14, 11:00) -> Loan Decision Approved (Jan 14, 12:00) -> Inform Applicant (Jan 16, 15:00) -> Finalize Doc (Jan 17, 09:00) -> Close Case (Jan 17, 10:00). No Follow-up Call. *   *Normative Check:* Sequence is App -> CC -> RA -> LD -> IA -> FD -> CC. Approved path followed perfectly. *   *Desiderata Check:* *   Credit Check within 24h? App at Jan 12 09:00, CC at Jan 14 10:00. Thats ~49 hours. -> **VIOLATION** (Desideratum). *   Inform within 48h of Decision? Decision Jan 14 12:00, Inform Jan 16 15:00. Thats 51 hours. -> **VIOLATION** (Desideratum). *   Follow-up Call? No. -> **VIOLATION** (Desideratum). *   *Result:* No normative violations. Multiple desideratum failures (timing delays, missing call).

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 5.  **Draft the Response Structure:** *   **Introduction:** Brief summary of the evaluation. *   **1. Normative Rule Violations:** Breakdown by Case. Clearly state the rule broken and the evidence from the log. *   **2. Desiderata Failures:** Breakdown by Case. Emphasize these are suboptimal but not severe. *   **3. Interpretations and Justifications:** Provide plausible real-world reasons for *why* these deviations might have occurred (e.g., system glitches, manual overrides, backlog, differing priorities).

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 6.  **Flesh out the Content (Iterative refinement):** *   *Drafting Section 1 (Normative):* ... *   *Drafting Section 2 (Desiderata):* ... *   *Drafting Section 3 (Interpretations/Justifications):* *   *Case 2 (The messy one):* Why do RA before CC? Maybe an automated trigger fired prematurely, or an experienced underwriter skipped ahead based on preliminary knowledge but logged it wrong. Why finalize after rejection? Possibly a systemic error where the documentation system wasnt notified of the rejection flag, or a manual data entry mistake. Why skip informing? Oversight due to the chaotic handling of the case. *   *Case 3 (The slow one):* Why the delays? Weekend effect! Jan 12 is a Friday. Jan 14 is a Sunday (or maybe Monday if the 13th is Sat). *Check calendar for 2024: Jan 12 is Friday, Jan 13 is Sat, Jan 14 is Sun.* Ah, weekend operations! This is a perfect justification for the 24h and 48h delays. Follow-up call skipped likely to just get the delayed case closed quickly.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7.  **Final Review against Constraints:** *   Did I answer all 3 parts of the prompt? Yes. *   Are the normative vs. desiderata distinctions clear? Yes. *   Is the tone appropriate? Yes.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 8.  **Final Polish:** (This leads to the provided good output, ensuring clear headings and bullet points for readability.)
